title: hibbertia circularis
intro: Hibbertia circularis, also known as the circular guinea flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. Native to the eastern coast of Australia, this plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine. The guinea flower is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may contribute to its use in treating various health issues, such as skin conditions and wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its effectiveness and safety for human use have not been extensively scientifically studied.
